# 1o1-utils

> Lightweight, tree-shakeable TypeScript utility library. 2 kB gzipped, zero dependencies.

Install: npm install 1o1-utils
Import: import { chunk, pick, slugify, retry } from "1o1-utils";
Individual: import { chunk } from "1o1-utils/chunk";

All functions use named object parameters: fn({ param1, param2 }).

---

## Arrays

### arrayToHash

Convert an array of objects into a hash map keyed by a property. Useful for fast lookups by a unique identifier.

Import: import { arrayToHash } from "1o1-utils/array-to-hash";

Signature:
function arrayToHash<T>({ array, key }: ArrayToHashParams<T>): Record<string, T>

Parameters:
- array (T[], required): The array of objects to convert
- key (keyof T, required): The property to use as hash key

Returns: Record<string, T>

Example:
const users = [
  { id: "a1", name: "Alice" },
  { id: "b2", name: "Bob" },
];
arrayToHash({ array: users, key: "id" });
// => { a1: { id: "a1", name: "Alice" }, b2: { id: "b2", name: "Bob" } }

Throws: Error if array is not an array. Error if key is not a string.
Skips items where the key value is falsy or not a string.

---

### chunk

Split an array into smaller arrays of a specified size. The last chunk may contain fewer elements.

Import: import { chunk } from "1o1-utils/chunk";

Signature:
function chunk<T>({ array, size }: ChunkParams<T>): T[][]

Parameters:
- array (T[], required): The array to split
- size (number, required): Max items per chunk (positive integer)

Returns: T[][]

Example:
chunk({ array: [1, 2, 3, 4, 5], size: 2 });
// => [[1, 2], [3, 4], [5]]

Throws: Error if array is not an array. Error if size is not a positive integer.
Returns [] for an empty array.

---

### groupBy

Group array elements into an object keyed by the value of a specified property.

Import: import { groupBy } from "1o1-utils/group-by";

Signature:
function groupBy<T>({ array, key }: GroupByParams<T>): Record<string, T[]>

Parameters:
- array (T[], required): The array to group
- key (keyof T, required): The property to group by

Returns: Record<string, T[]>

Example:
const items = [
  { name: "Alice", role: "admin" },
  { name: "Bob", role: "user" },
  { name: "Carol", role: "admin" },
];
groupBy({ array: items, key: "role" });
// => { admin: [{ name: "Alice", role: "admin" }, { name: "Carol", role: "admin" }], user: [{ name: "Bob", role: "user" }] }

Throws: Error if array is not an array. Error if key is undefined or null.
Groups are keyed by String(value[key]).

---

### sortBy

Return a new array sorted by a specified property. Supports ascending/descending and dot notation for nested properties.

Import: import { sortBy } from "1o1-utils/sort-by";

Signature:
function sortBy<T>({ array, key, order = "asc" }: SortByParams<T>): T[]

Parameters:
- array (T[], required): The array to sort
- key (keyof T | string, required): Property to sort by (supports dot notation)
- order ("asc" | "desc", optional): Sort direction (default: "asc")

Returns: T[] — A new sorted array (does not mutate the original).

Example:
sortBy({ array: [{ age: 30 }, { age: 20 }], key: "age" });
// => [{ age: 20 }, { age: 30 }]

sortBy({ array: [{ age: 30 }, { age: 20 }], key: "age", order: "desc" });
// => [{ age: 30 }, { age: 20 }]

sortBy({ array: users, key: "address.city" }); // nested dot notation

Throws: Error if array is not an array. Error if key is undefined or null.
Supports both numeric and string comparisons.

---

### unique

Return a new array with duplicate elements removed. For objects, specify a key to determine uniqueness.

Import: import { unique } from "1o1-utils/unique";

Signature:
function unique<T>({ array, key }: UniqueParams<T>): T[]

Parameters:
- array (T[], required): The array to deduplicate
- key (keyof T, optional): Property to determine uniqueness for objects

Returns: T[]

Example:
unique({ array: [1, 2, 2, 3, 1] });
// => [1, 2, 3]

unique({ array: [{ id: 1, name: "Alice" }, { id: 2, name: "Bob" }, { id: 1, name: "Alice Copy" }], key: "id" });
// => [{ id: 1, name: "Alice" }, { id: 2, name: "Bob" }]

Throws: Error if array is not an array.
Without key, uses Set for primitive deduplication. With key, keeps the first occurrence.

---

## Objects

### cloneDeep

Create a deep clone of any value. Handles plain objects, arrays, Date, RegExp, Map, Set, typed arrays, ArrayBuffer, Error, and circular references. Functions are copied by reference.

Import: import { cloneDeep } from "1o1-utils/clone-deep";

Signature:
function cloneDeep<T>({ value }: CloneDeepParams<T>): T

Parameters:
- value (T, required): The value to clone

Returns: T — A deep clone of the input. The return type matches the input type.

Example:
const original = { a: { b: [1, 2] }, date: new Date() };
const cloned = cloneDeep({ value: original });
cloned.a.b.push(3);
original.a.b; // [1, 2] — unaffected

Primitives are returned as-is. Functions are copied by reference. Map values are deep cloned but keys are kept by reference. Circular references are detected via a WeakMap. Uses an iterative, stack-based algorithm to avoid stack overflow on deeply nested structures. Custom class prototype chains are not preserved.

---

### deepMerge

Recursively merge two objects into a new object. Nested plain objects are merged deeply; arrays and other types are overwritten.

Import: import { deepMerge } from "1o1-utils/deep-merge";

Signature:
function deepMerge({ target, source }: DeepMergeParams): Record<string, unknown>

Parameters:
- target (Record<string, unknown>, required): The base object
- source (Record<string, unknown>, required): The object to merge into target

Returns: Record<string, unknown> — A new merged object (neither input is mutated).

Example:
deepMerge({
  target: { a: 1, b: { x: 10 } },
  source: { b: { y: 20 }, c: 3 },
});
// => { a: 1, b: { x: 10, y: 20 }, c: 3 }

Throws: Error if target is not a plain object. Error if source is not a plain object.
Uses a stack-based (non-recursive) algorithm to avoid stack overflow on deeply nested objects.

---

### isEmpty

Check whether a given value is considered empty. Supports strings, arrays, plain objects, Maps, and Sets.

Import: import { isEmpty } from "1o1-utils/is-empty";

Signature:
function isEmpty({ value }: IsEmptyParams): boolean

Parameters:
- value (unknown, required): The value to check

Returns: boolean

What counts as empty: null, undefined, "" (empty string), [] (empty array), {} (empty plain object), empty Map, empty Set.
What is NOT empty: 0, false, class instances, non-plain objects.

Example:
isEmpty({ value: "" });        // => true
isEmpty({ value: [] });        // => true
isEmpty({ value: {} });        // => true
isEmpty({ value: null });      // => true
isEmpty({ value: new Map() }); // => true
isEmpty({ value: "hello" });   // => false
isEmpty({ value: [1, 2] });    // => false
isEmpty({ value: 0 });         // => false
isEmpty({ value: false });     // => false

---

### omit

Create a new object with the specified keys removed. Supports dot notation for nested keys.

Import: import { omit } from "1o1-utils/omit";

Signature:
function omit<T extends Record<string, unknown>>({ obj, keys }: OmitParams<T>): Record<string, unknown>

Parameters:
- obj (T, required): The source object
- keys ((keyof T | string)[], required): Keys to exclude (supports dot notation)

Returns: Record<string, unknown>

Example:
omit({ obj: { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 }, keys: ["b", "c"] });
// => { a: 1 }

omit({ obj: { a: 1, b: { x: 10, y: 20 } }, keys: ["b.x"] });
// => { a: 1, b: { y: 20 } }

Throws: Error if obj is not an object. Error if keys is not an array.
Does not mutate the original object.

---

### pick

Create a new object containing only the specified keys. Supports dot notation for nested keys.

Import: import { pick } from "1o1-utils/pick";

Signature:
function pick<T extends Record<string, unknown>>({ obj, keys }: PickParams<T>): Record<string, unknown>

Parameters:
- obj (T, required): The source object
- keys ((keyof T | string)[], required): Keys to include (supports dot notation)

Returns: Record<string, unknown>

Example:
pick({ obj: { a: 1, b: 2, c: 3 }, keys: ["a", "c"] });
// => { a: 1, c: 3 }

pick({ obj: { a: 1, b: { x: 10, y: 20 } }, keys: ["a", "b.x"] });
// => { a: 1, b: { x: 10 } }

Throws: Error if obj is not an object. Error if keys is not an array.
Missing keys are silently skipped.

---

## Strings

### capitalize

Capitalize the first character of a string, with an option to preserve or lowercase the rest.

Import: import { capitalize } from "1o1-utils/capitalize";

Signature:
function capitalize({ str, preserveRest }: CapitalizeParams): string

Parameters:
- str (string, required): The string to capitalize
- preserveRest (boolean, optional): If true, keeps the rest as-is; if false, lowercases it (default: false)

Returns: string

Example:
capitalize({ str: "hello" });                    // => "Hello"
capitalize({ str: "hELLO", preserveRest: true }); // => "HELLO"
capitalize({ str: "hELLO" });                     // => "Hello"

Throws: Error if str is not a string.
Returns "" for empty string.

---

### slugify

Convert a string to a URL-friendly slug by lowercasing, stripping accents, and replacing non-alphanumeric characters with hyphens.

Import: import { slugify } from "1o1-utils/slugify";

Signature:
function slugify({ str }: SlugifyParams): string

Parameters:
- str (string, required): The string to slugify

Returns: string

Example:
slugify({ str: "Hello World!" });             // => "hello-world"
slugify({ str: "Cafe au lait" });             // => "cafe-au-lait"
slugify({ str: "  --Multiple   Spaces--  " }); // => "multiple-spaces"

Throws: Error if str is not a string.
Handles Unicode normalization (NFD) to strip accents. Removes leading/trailing hyphens. Collapses multiple non-alphanumeric characters into a single hyphen.

---

### transformCase

Convert a string between common case styles: camelCase, kebab-case, snake_case, and PascalCase.

Import: import { transformCase } from "1o1-utils/transform-case";

Signature:
function transformCase({ str, to }: TransformCaseParams): string

Parameters:
- str (string, required): The string to convert
- to ("camel" | "kebab" | "snake" | "pascal", required): Target case style

Returns: string

Example:
transformCase({ str: "hello world", to: "camel" });  // => "helloWorld"
transformCase({ str: "hello world", to: "kebab" });  // => "hello-world"
transformCase({ str: "hello world", to: "snake" });  // => "hello_world"
transformCase({ str: "hello world", to: "pascal" }); // => "HelloWorld"
transformCase({ str: "myVariableName", to: "kebab" }); // => "my-variable-name"

Throws: Error if str is not a string. Error if to is not one of: camel, kebab, snake, pascal.
Splits on word boundaries (uppercase transitions, hyphens, underscores, spaces).

---

### truncate

Truncate a string to a specified length, appending a configurable suffix.

Import: import { truncate } from "1o1-utils/truncate";

Signature:
function truncate({ str, length, suffix }: TruncateParams): string

Parameters:
- str (string, required): The string to truncate
- length (number, required): Maximum length (positive integer)
- suffix (string, optional): String appended when truncated (default: "...")

Returns: string

Example:
truncate({ str: "Hello, World!", length: 5 });              // => "Hello..."
truncate({ str: "Hello, World!", length: 5, suffix: "..." }); // => "Hello..."
truncate({ str: "Hi", length: 10 });                         // => "Hi"

Throws: Error if str is not a string. Error if length is not a positive integer.
Returns the original string if its length does not exceed length. The suffix is appended beyond the length limit.

---

## Async

### debounce

Create a debounced version of a function that delays invocation until after a specified number of milliseconds have elapsed since the last call.

Import: import { debounce } from "1o1-utils/debounce";

Signature:
function debounce<T>({ fn, ms }: DebounceParams<T>): Debounced<T>

Parameters:
- fn (T, required): The function to debounce
- ms (number, required): Delay in milliseconds (non-negative)

Returns: Debounced<T> — The debounced function with a .cancel() method.

Example:
const debouncedSearch = debounce({
  fn: (query: string) => search(query),
  ms: 300,
});
input.addEventListener("input", (e) => debouncedSearch(e.target.value));
debouncedSearch.cancel(); // Cancel pending execution

Throws: Error if fn is not a function. Error if ms is not a number, is NaN, or is negative.
Each call resets the delay timer. .cancel() clears any pending timeout.

---

### retry

Retry an async (or sync) function up to a specified number of attempts, with configurable delay and backoff strategy.

Import: import { retry } from "1o1-utils/retry";

Signature:
async function retry<T>({ fn, attempts, delay, backoff, onRetry }: RetryParams<T>): Promise<T>

Parameters:
- fn (() => Promise<T> | T, required): The function to retry
- attempts (number, optional): Maximum attempts (positive integer, default: 3)
- delay (number, optional): Delay between retries in ms (default: 1000)
- backoff ("fixed" | "exponential", optional): Backoff strategy (default: "fixed")
- onRetry ((error, attempt) => void, optional): Callback invoked on each retry

Returns: Promise<T> — The result of the first successful call.

Example:
const data = await retry({
  fn: () => fetch("/api/data").then((r) => r.json()),
  attempts: 5,
  delay: 2000,
  backoff: "exponential",
});

await retry({
  fn: fetchData,
  onRetry: (error, attempt) => console.log(`Attempt ${attempt} failed:`, error),
});

Throws: Error if fn is not a function. Error if attempts is not a positive integer. Error if delay is negative or NaN. Error if backoff is not "fixed" or "exponential".
With exponential backoff, delay is delay * 2^i for attempt i. Throws the last error after all attempts are exhausted.

---

### sleep

Pause execution for a given number of milliseconds by returning a promise.

Import: import { sleep } from "1o1-utils/sleep";

Signature:
function sleep({ ms }: SleepParams): Promise<void>

Parameters:
- ms (number, required): Duration in milliseconds (non-negative)

Returns: Promise<void>

Example:
await sleep({ ms: 1000 }); // waits 1 second

Throws: Error if ms is not a number or is NaN. Error if ms is negative.
sleep({ ms: 0 }) resolves on the next tick.

---

### throttle

Create a throttled version of a function that only invokes the original at most once per specified interval.

Import: import { throttle } from "1o1-utils/throttle";

Signature:
function throttle<T>({ fn, ms }: ThrottleParams<T>): Throttled<T>

Parameters:
- fn (T, required): The function to throttle
- ms (number, required): Minimum interval between calls in milliseconds (non-negative)

Returns: Throttled<T> — The throttled function with a .cancel() method.

Example:
const throttledScroll = throttle({
  fn: () => updatePosition(),
  ms: 100,
});
window.addEventListener("scroll", throttledScroll);
throttledScroll.cancel(); // Cancel pending trailing call

Throws: Error if fn is not a function. Error if ms is not a number, is NaN, or is negative.
First call executes immediately. Subsequent calls within ms are queued; the last one fires after the interval. .cancel() clears pending calls and resets state.
